author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the same words of the Gospel, John xiv. 6, ‘I am the way,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the same words of the Gospel, John xiv. 6, ‘I am the way,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The divine lessons raise us up, that we be not broken by despair; and terrify us again, that we be not tossed to and fro by pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to hold the middle, the true, the strait way, as it were between the left hand of despair, and the right hand of presumption, would be most difficult for us, had not Christ said, “I am the Way, and the Truth, and the Life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As if He had said, “By what way wouldest thou go?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘I am the Way’.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whither wouldest thou go?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘I am the Truth.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where wouldest thou abide?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘I am the Life.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then walk with all assurance in the Way; but let us fear snares by the way side.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The enemy does not dare to lay his snares in the way; because Christ is the Way; but most certainly by the way side he ceases not to do so.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence too it is said in the Psalm, “They have laid stumblingblocks for me by the way side.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And another Scripture saith, “Remember that thou walkest in the midst of snares.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These snares among which we walk are not in the way; but yet they are “by the way side.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What fearest thou, what art thou alarmed at, so thou walk in the Way?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fear then, if thou forsake the Way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For for this reason is the enemy even permitted to lay snares by the way side, lest through the security of exultation the Way be forsaken, and ye fall into the snares.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ Humbled is the Way; Christ the Truth and the Life, Christ Highly Exalted and God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou walk in the Humbled, thou shalt attain to the Exalted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If infirm as thou art, thou despise not the Humbled, thou shalt abide exceeding strong in the Exalted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what cause was there of Christ’s Humiliation, save thine infirmity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For solely and irremediably did thine infirmity press thee in, and this circumstance it was that made so great a Physician come to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thy sickness had been even such, that thou couldest have gone to the Physician, this infirmity might have seemed endurable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because thou couldest not go to Him, He came to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came teaching humility, whereby we might return; for that pride allowed us not to return to life; yea had even made us depart from life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the heart of man being lifted up against God, and neglecting in its sound state His saving precepts, the soul fell away into infirmity; let her in her infirmity learn to hear Him whom in her strength she despised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let her hear Him that she may rise, whom she despised, that she might fall.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let her at length, taught by experience, give ear to what she had no mind, when taught by precept, to obtain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For her misery hath taught her, how evil a thing it is to go a whoring from the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to fall away from that Simple and Singular Good, into this multitude of pleasures, into the love of the world, and earthly corruption, is to go a whoring from the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He hath addressed her as in a sense a harlot, to warn her to return: very often by the Prophets doth He reproach her as a harlot, but yet not despaired of, for that He who reproacheth the harlot hath in His Hands the cleansing of the harlot too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He doth not so reproach as to insult her; but He would bring her to confusion of face to heal her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Vehement are the exclamations of Scripture, nor doth it deal softly by flattery with those whom it would by healing recover.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Ye adulterers, know ye not that the friend of this world is constituted the enemy of God?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The love of the world maketh the soul adulterous, the love of the Framer of the world maketh the soul chaste; but unless she blush for her corruption, she hath no desire to return to that chaste embrace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be she confounded that she may return, who was vaunting herself that she should not return.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was pride then that hindered the soul’s return.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whoso reproacheth doth not cause the sin, but showeth the sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the soul was loth to see, is placed before her eyes; and what she desired to have behind her back, is brought before her face.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See thyself in thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Why seest thou the mote in thy brother’s eye, but perceivest not the beam in thine own eye?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The soul which went away from herself, is recalled to herself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As she had gone away from herself, so went she away from her Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For she had respect to herself, and pleased herself, and became enamoured of her own power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She withdrew from him, and abode not in herself; and from her own self she is repelled, and from herself shut out, and she falleth away unto things without her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She loves the world, loves the things of time, loves earthly things; who if she but loved herself to the neglect of Him by whom she was made, would at once be less, at once fail by loving that which is less.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For she is less than God; yea less by far, and by so much less as the thing made is less than the Maker.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was God then That ought to have been loved, yea in such wise ought God to be loved, that if it might be so, we should forget ourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is this change?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The soul hath forgotten herself, but by loving the world; let her now forget herself, but by loving the world’s Maker.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Driven away even from herself, I say, she hath in a manner lost herself, and hath not skilled to see her own actions, she justifies her iniquities; she is puffed up, and prides herself in insolence, in voluptuousness, in honours, in posts of authority, in riches, in the power of vanity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She is reproved, rebuked, is shown to herself, mislikes herself, confesses her deformity, longs for her first beauty, and she who went away in profusion returns in confusion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seemeth he to pray against her, or for her, who says, “Fill their faces with shame”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It seems to be an adversary, it seems an enemy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear what follows, and see whether a friend can offer this prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Fill,” says he, “their faces with shame, and they shall seek Thy Name, O Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did he hate them whose faces he desired to be filled with shame?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See how he loves them whom he would have seek the Name of the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does he love only, or hate only? or does he both hate, and love?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, he both hates, and loves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hates what is thine, he loves thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “He hates what is thine, he loves thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hates what thou hast made, he loves what God hath made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what are thine own things but sins?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what art thou but what God made thee, a man after His Own image and likeness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost neglect what thou wast made, love what thou hast made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost love thine own works without thee, dost neglect the work of God within thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Deservedly dost thou go away, deservedly fall off, yea, deservedly even from thine own self depart; deservedly hear the words, “A spirit that goeth and returneth not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear rather Him That calleth and saith, “Turn ye unto Me, and I will turn unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For God doth not really turn away, and turn again; Abiding the Same He rebuketh, Unchangeable He rebuketh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath turned away, in that thou hast turned thyself away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast fallen from Him, He hath not fallen away from thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear Him then saying to thee, “Turn ye unto Me, and I will turn unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this is, “I turn unto you, in that ye turn unto Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He followeth on the back of him that flieth, He enlighteneth the face of him that returneth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whither wilt thou fly in flying from God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whither wilt thou fly in flying from Him who is contained in no place, and is nowhere absent?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He That delivereth him that turneth to him, punisheth him that turneth away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast a Judge by flying; have a Father by returning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he had been swollen up by pride, and by this swelling could not return by the strait way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who became the Way, crieth out, “Enter ye in by the strait gate.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He tries to enter in, the swelling impedes him; and his trying is so much the more hurtful, in proportion as the swelling is a greater impediment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the straitness irritates his swelling; and being irritated he will swell the more; and swelling more, when will he enter in?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then let him bring down the swelling.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him take the medicine of humility; let him against the swelling drink the bitter but wholesome cup; drink the cup of humility.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why doth he squeeze himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The bulk, not for its size, but for its swelling, doth not allow him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For size hath solidity, swelling inflation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not him that is swollen fancy himself of great size; that he may be great, and substantial, and solid, let him bring down his swelling.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him not long after these present things, let him not glory in this pomp of things failing and corruptible; let him hearken to Him who said, “Enter in by the strait gate,” saying also, “I am the Way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as if some swollen one had asked, “How shall I enter in?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saith, “‘I am the Way.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Enter in by Me; Thou walkest only by Me, to enter in by the door.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as He said, “I am the Way;” so also, “I am the Door.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why seekest thou whereby to return, whither to return, whereby to enter in?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lest thou shouldest in any respect go astray, He became all for thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore in brief He saith, “Be humble, be meek.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us hear Him saying this most plainly, that thou mayest see whereby is the way, what is the way, whither is the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whither wouldest thou come?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But peradventure in covetousness thou wouldest possess all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All things are delivered unto Me of My Father,” saith He.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It may be thou wilt say, “They were delivered to Christ: but are they to me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle speak; hear, as I said some time ago, lest thou be broken by despair; hear how thou wert loved when thou hadst nothing to be loved for, hear how thou wert loved when unsightly, deformed, before there was ought in thee which was meet to be loved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wast first loved, that thou mightest be made meet to be loved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Christ, as the Apostle says, “died for the ungodly.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! will you say that the ungodly deserved to be loved?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask, what did the ungodly deserve?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To be damned.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here you will answer, Yet, “Christ died for the ungodly.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, what was done for thee when ungodly; what is reserved for thee now godly?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Christ died for the ungodly.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou didst desire to possess all things; desire it not through covetousness, seek it through piety, seek it through humility.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou seek thus, thou shalt possess.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou shalt have Him by whom all things were made, and with Him shalt possess all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not say this as though the result of reasoning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle himself saying, “He that spared not His Own Son, but delivered Him up for us all; how hath He also not with Him given us all things?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, covetous one, thou hast all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All things that thou lovest, despise, that thou be not kept back from Christ, and hold to Him in whom thou mayest possess all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Physician Himself then needing no such medicine, yet that He might encourage the sick, drank what He had no need of; addressing him as it were refusing it, and raising him up in his fear, He drank first.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Cup,” saith He, “which I shall drink of;” “I who have nothing in Me to be cured by that Cup, am yet to drink it, that thou who needest to drink it, may not disdain to drink.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now consider, Brethren, ought the human race to be any longer sick after having received such a medicine?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God hath been now Humbled, and is man still proud?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him hear, let him learn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All things,” saith He, have been delivered unto Me of My Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou desirest all things, thou shalt have them with Me; if thou desirest the Father, by Me and in Me thou shalt have Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “No man knoweth the Father but the Son.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not despair; come to the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear what follows, “And he to whom the Son will reveal Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou saidst, “I am not able.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou callest me through a strait way; I am not able to enter in by a strait way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Come,” saith He, “unto Me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Your burden is your swelling.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Come unto Me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will refresh you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take My yoke upon you, and learn of Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Master of the Angels crieth out, the Word of God, by whom all reasonable souls are without failing fed, the Food That refresheth, and abideth Entire, crieth out and saith, “Learn of Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the people hear Him, saying, “Learn of Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them make answer, “What do we learn of Thee?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we must be going to hear I know not what from the Great Artificer, when He saith, “Learn of Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is it that saith, “Learn of Me”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who formed the earth, who divided the sea and the dry land, who created the fowls, who created the animals of the earth, who created all things that swim, who set the stars in the heaven, who distinguished the day and the night, who established the firmament, who separated the light from the darkness, He it is who saith, “Learn of Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is He haply about to tell us this, that we should do these things with Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can do this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God Only doeth them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Fear not,” He saith, “I am not laying any burden on thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘Learn of Me,’ this which for thy sake I was made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘Learn of Me,’” saith He, “not to form the creature which by Me was made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Neither do I tell you indeed, to learn those things which I have granted to some, to whom I would, not to all, to raise the dead, to give sight to the blind, to open the ears of the deaf; nor to wish as for some great thing to learn these things of Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The disciples returned with joy and exultation, saying, “Lo, even the devils are subject unto us through Thy Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord said to them, “In this rejoice not, that the devils are subject unto you; rejoice rather, because your names are written in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom He would, He gave the power to cast out devils, to whom He would, He gave the power to raise the dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such miracles were done even before the Incarnation of the Lord; the dead were raised, lepers were cleansed; we read of these things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who did them then, but He who in after time was the Man-Christ after David, but God-Christ before Abraham?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He gave the power for all these things, He did them Himself by men; yet gave He not that power to all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ought they to whom He gave it not to despair, and say that they have no part in Him because they have not been thought worthy to receive these gifts?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the body are divers members: this member can do one thing, that another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God hath compacted the body together, He hath not given to the ear to see, nor to the eye to hear, nor to the forehead to smell, nor to the hand to taste; He hath not given them these functions; but to all the members hath He given soundness, hath given union, hath given unity, hath by His Spirit quickened and united all alike.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so here He hath not given to some to raise the dead, to others He hath not given the power of disputation; yet to all what hath He given?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Learn of Me, that I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forasmuch as we have heard Him say, “I am meek and lowly in heart;” here, my Brethren, is our whole remedy, “Learn of Me, that I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What doth it profit a man if he do miracles, and is proud, is not meek and lowly in heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Will he not be reckoned in the number of those who shall come at the last day, and say, “Have we not prophesied in Thy Name, and in Thy Name have done many mighty works?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what shall they hear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I know you not, Depart from Me, all ye that work iniquity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then doth it profit us to learn?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That I am meek,” saith He, “and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He engrafteth charity, and that most genuine charity, without confusion, without inflation, without elation, without deceit; this doth He engraft, who saith, “Learn of Me, that I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How can one proud and puffed up have any genuine charity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He must needs be envious.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And mayhap one who is envious, loves, and we are mistaken?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God forbid that any one should be so mistaken, as to say that an envious man hath charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so what saith the Apostle?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Charity envieth not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why doth it not envy?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “It is not puffed up;” he immediately annexed the cause for which he took away envying from charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because it is not puffed up, it envieth not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is true, he said first, “Charity envieth not;” but as though thou didst ask, “Why doth it not envy?” he added, “It is not puffed up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then it envieth because it is puffed up; if it be not puffed up, it envieth not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If charity is not puffed up, and therefore envieth not; then doth He engraft charity who saith, “Learn of Me, that I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let any man have then what he will, let him boast himself of what he will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If I speak with the tongues of men and of Angels, but, have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is more sublime than the gift of divers tongues?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is “brass,” it is “a tinkling cymbal,” if thou take charity away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear other gifts; “If I should know all mysteries.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more excellent? what more magnificent?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear yet another; “if I should have all prophecy, and all faith, so that I could remove mountains, but have not charity, I am nothing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He comes to still greater things, Brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What else has he said?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If I should distribute all my goods to the poor.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more perfect thing can be done?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When indeed the Lord commanded the rich man this for perfection’s sake, saying, “If thou wilt be perfect, go, sell all that thou hast, and give to the poor.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was he then at once perfect, because he sold all his goods and gave them to the poor?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No; and therefore He added, “And come, follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Sell all,” saith He, “give to the poor, and come, follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Why should I follow Thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now that I have sold all, and distributed to the poor, am I not perfect?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What need is there that I should follow Thee?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Follow Me,” that thou mayest learn that “I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what? can any man sell all he hath, and give to the poor, who is not yet meek, not yet lowly in heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Assuredly he can.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For if I should distribute all my goods to the poor.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And hear still further.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For some, who had left all they had and had already followed the Lord, but not yet followed Him perfectly (for to follow Him perfectly is to imitate Him), could not bear the trial of suffering.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter, Brethren, was already one of those who had left all and followed the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as that rich man went away in sadness, when the disciples bring troubled, asked how then any one could be perfect, and the Lord consoled them, they said to the Lord, “Behold, we have forsaken all, and followed Thee; what shall we have therefore?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord told them what He would give them here, what He would reserve for them hereafter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now Peter was already of the number of those who had so done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when it came to the crisis of suffering, at the voice of a maid-servant he denied Him thrice with whom he had promised that he was ready to die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take good heed then, Beloved: “Go,” saith He, “sell all that thou hast, give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven, and come, follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter is perfect, now that the Lord sitteth in heaven at the right Hand of the Father, then did he attain perfection and maturity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when he followed the Lord to His Passion, he was not perfect; but when there began to be no one on earth for him to follow, then was he perfected.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou truly hast always One before thee to follow; the Lord hath set up an example on earth, when He left the Gospel with thee, in the Gospel He is with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He did not speak falsely when He said, “Lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore follow the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “Follow the Lord”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Imitate the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “Imitate the Lord”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Learn of Me, that I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because if I should distribute all my goods to the poor, and give up my body to be burned, but not have charity, it profiteth me nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To this charity then I exhort your Charity; now I should not exhort to charity, but with some charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I exhort then that what is commenced may be filled up; and pray that what is begun may be perfected.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And I beg that ye would offer this prayer for me, that what I advise may be perfected in me also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we are all now imperfect, and there shall we be perfected, where all things are perfect.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul says, “Brethren, I do not reckon myself to have apprehended.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He says, “Not that I have already attained, either am already perfect.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And shall any man dare to vaunt himself on perfection?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea rather let us acknowledge our imperfection, that we may attain perfection.
